title: Interpret A Green AGV State As Ready But Without A Current Task
summary: Use this source-backed reference to interpret an AGV shown in green in the interface as ready to execute but not currently assigned a task. The same source also notes that an AGV not in a task can be manually repositioned with the Go to function instead of physically pushing it.
when_to_use: Use when viewing AGV status in the interface and you need to determine what a green AGV state means, or when deciding whether an idle AGV may be repositioned using the Go to function.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to interpret AGV states other than green.
Do not use this runbook to infer a full AGV status table or undocumented meanings.
Do not use this runbook as authority to physically push an AGV; the source specifically describes using the Go to function for an AGV not in a task.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the AGV state in the interface, interpret a green state using the source-backed meaning, and communicate or record that the AGV is ready but currently has no task.

safety_notes: Use the Go to function for manual repositioning of an AGV that is not in a task rather than physically pushing it, as described in the source.
Do not infer meanings for other AGV colors from this runbook because this candidate is limited to the documented green-state interpretation.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the AGV status display or interface

access_required: Ability to view the AGV in the OptiSweep interface or AGV status display
system_preconditions: The AGV state must be visible in the interface
If using Go to, the AGV must not currently be in a task

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript segment describing the AGV Go to function and stating that a green AGV is ready to execute but has no task.

steps.title: Observe the AGV state in the interface
steps.purpose: Identify whether the AGV is currently shown in the green state.
steps.instruction: View the AGV in the interface or status display and determine whether the AGV is shown in green.
steps.expected_result: The operator identifies whether the AGV is displayed in green.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is clearly visible in the interface and its state color can be observed.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV state cannot be seen clearly, is not green, or the display is ambiguous.

actions.label: Check AGV color state
actions.exact_action: Observe the AGV state in the interface and identify whether it is shown in green.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the AGV state discussion and Go to function context tied to an AGV that is in green and not in a task.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed AGV state cannot be determined from the interface.
The AGV is not shown in green and the operator needs a different state interpretation.

source_evidence.text: Observe the AGV state in the interface and identify whether it is shown in green.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Green indicates the AGV is ready to execute but currently has no task.

steps.title: Interpret the green state using the source meaning
steps.purpose: Apply the documented meaning of the green AGV state from the source.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed green AGV state to the source statement and interpret it as meaning the AGV is ready to execute something but does not currently have a task.
steps.expected_result: The operator correctly interprets the green AGV as ready but without a current task.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed green state matches the documented source meaning of ready but no task.
steps.failure_condition: The observed state appears inconsistent with the documented meaning or the operator is unsure whether the source applies.

actions.label: Apply green-state meaning
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed green state to the source statement that green means the AGV is ready to execute something but does not have a task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the training frame and transcript segment that explains the green AGV state meaning.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed AGV state does not match the documented green-state meaning in the source.
Additional interpretation is needed for a non-green state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If an AGV is in the green state, that means it is ready to execute something, but it does not have a task.
The transcript explains that if an AGV is in a green state and ready but has no task...

steps.title: Record or communicate the AGV status
steps.purpose: Ensure the interpreted AGV state is conveyed consistently for operations or support follow-up.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate that the AGV is ready but currently idle with no task assigned.
steps.expected_result: The AGV status is communicated as ready but without a current task.
steps.healthy_condition: Operators or support personnel understand that the AGV is available and not currently executing a task.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV is misreported as faulted, disconnected, or actively in task when the source-supported interpretation is green and no task.

actions.label: Communicate interpreted state
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate that the AGV is ready but currently idle with no task assigned.

stop_or_escalate_if: There is disagreement about the meaning of the displayed green state.
The AGV behavior appears inconsistent with being ready but without a task.

source_evidence.text: The user can correctly interpret a green AGV state as ready to execute but not currently assigned a task.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Green indicates the AGV is ready to execute but currently has no task.

steps.title: Use Go to for manual repositioning if needed
steps.purpose: Reposition an AGV that is not in a task using the source-described interface function instead of physically moving it.
steps.instruction: If repositioning is needed and the AGV is not in a task, use the Go to function described in the source. Select an AGV, select Go to, select which square to send the AGV to, and select Confirm.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is manually directed to a selected square or map cell using the Go to function.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is not in a task and can be sent to another square or map cell through the interface.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV is in a task, the Go to function cannot be completed, or repositioning is attempted by physically pushing the AGV instead of using the documented function.

actions.label: Choose destination square
actions.exact_action: Select which square to send the AGV to.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the Robot Selection Go to function steps and the explanation that this avoids pushing the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV is currently in a task.
The Go to function is unavailable or does not behave as described.
The operator is being asked to infer additional recovery actions not documented in this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Go-to function is helpful because it saves the operator from pushing the AGV; an AGV not in a task can be sent to another square or map cell.
The slide OCR lists steps: select an AGV, select Go to, select which square to send the AGV to, and select Confirm.

success_criteria: A green AGV state is interpreted as ready to execute but currently without a task.
The operator does not infer unsupported meanings for other AGV states.
If repositioning is needed, the operator uses the Go to function for an AGV not in a task.
healthy_conditions: The AGV is shown in green in the interface.
The green state aligns with the source-backed meaning of ready but no current task.
The Go to function is available for an AGV that is not in a task.
failure_conditions: The AGV display does not match the documented green-state meaning.
The operator attempts to interpret undocumented AGV states using this runbook.
The AGV is repositioned by unsupported means instead of the documented Go to function.
escalation_guidance: Seek additional support if the displayed AGV state does not match the documented green-state meaning in the source.
Escalate if a broader AGV state interpretation is needed because this source segment only documents the green state.
Escalate if the AGV is not in a task but the Go to function cannot be used as described.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Green AGV state interpretation and Robot Selection Go to function steps.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide a direct screenshot solely of the green state indicator; the attached artifact is the closest source-backed visual reference.
The source does not provide a formal logging method, destination system, or communication channel for recording the interpreted AGV status.
The source does not provide timing estimates, role boundaries beyond operator use, or production-stop/LOTO requirements for this reference procedure.
